# Spec context: §5.3 — Numbering

## Source

- Upstream: <https://abcnotation.com/wiki/abc:standard:v2.1#numbering>

- Subsection: 5.3 Numbering

## Preceding prose

> *VOLATILE:* The following syntax may be extended to include non-numeric "numbering".
> If the first word of a `w:` line starts with a digit, this is interpreted as numbering of a stanza. Typesetting programs should align the corresponding note with the first letter that occurs. This can be used in conjunction with the `~` symbol mentioned in the table above to create a space between t
> *Example:* In the following, the `1.~Three` is treated as a single word with a space created by the `~`, but the fact that the `w:` line starts with a number means that the first note of the corresponding music line is aligned to `Three`.
